title: Budget 2026: Expectations High for Personal Tax Relief
author: Economy Desk
content: New Delhi: With the Union Budget 2026 approaching, middle-class taxpayers are holding out hope for significant relief, specifically expecting an increase in the standard deduction limits and revisions to the tax slabs.

Industry bodies and tax experts have submitted pre-budget memorandums suggesting that adjusting the tax brackets for inflation could boost disposable income, thereby stimulating consumption and overall economic growth.

Currently, the standard deduction stands at a level that many argue has not kept pace with rising living costs. There is also a strong demand for enhanced deductions under Section 80C to encourage long-term savings.

However, the Finance Ministry faces the delicate task of balancing these populist measures with the imperative of maintaining fiscal deficit targets. Revenue collections have been robust, providing some headroom, but global uncertainties urge caution.
